document.title: Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6 README
productModels: Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6

sourceSummary.oneLine: README for Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6 describing deployment, redistribution, optional files, cryptography policy files, certificates, and endorsed standards override.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides Oracle redistribution and deployment guidance for vendors bundling the Java SE Runtime Environment with applications.
keyPoints: The JRE is intended for software developers and vendors to redistribute with their applications, excluding the JavaFX runtime.
Redistribution is subject to the Software License Agreement and listed obligations, restrictions, and exceptions.
All files not listed as optional files must be included in redistributions of the runtime environment.
The document describes optional redistributable files, limited redistributable JDK files, JCE policy files, the cacerts certificate file, and the Endorsed Standards Override Mechanism.

entities.name: Oracle
entities.evidence: The Java(TM) Runtime Environment (JRE) and the JavaFX(TM) runtime are products of Oracle.

entities.name: Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment
entities.evidence: Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6

entities.name: JavaFX(TM) runtime
entities.evidence: The JRE installation triggers the download of the JavaFX runtime. The JavaFX runtime is also available separately, and is not part of the JRE.

entities.name: Java SE Development Kit (JDK(TM))
entities.evidence: For development tools, see the Java SE Development Kit (JDK(TM)).

entities.name: Java Community Process(SM)
entities.evidence: standards that are created outside of the Java Community Process(SM) (JCP(SM) http://www.jcp.org/)

concepts.name: Java SE Runtime Environment redistribution
concepts.evidence: You may reproduce and distribute the Software (and also portions of Software identified below as Redistributable), provided that:

concepts.name: Required vs. Optional Files
concepts.pageTarget: Java SE Runtime Environment required and optional files
concepts.evidence: The files that make up the Java SE Runtime Environment are divided into two categories: required and optional.

concepts.name: Unlimited Strength Java Cryptography Extension
concepts.evidence: An unlimited strength version of these files indicating no restrictions on cryptographic strengths is available on the JDK web site for those living in eligible countries.

concepts.name: cacerts Certificates File
concepts.evidence: Root CA certificates may be added to or removed from the Java SE certificate file located at <java-home>/lib/security/cacerts

concepts.name: Endorsed Standards Override Mechanism
concepts.evidence: The Endorsed Standards Override Mechanism provides a means whereby later versions of classes and interfaces that implement Endorsed Standards or Standalone Technologies may be incorporated into the Java Platform.

evidence.claim: The document is for Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6.
evidence.evidenceText: Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment Version 6

evidence.claim: The JRE is intended for developers and vendors to redistribute with applications, excluding the JavaFX runtime.
evidence.evidenceText: The Java(TM) Platform, Standard Edition Runtime Environment (JRE(TM)), excluding the JavaFX(TM) runtime, is intended for software developers and vendors to redistribute with their applications.

evidence.claim: The Java SE Runtime Environment is not a development environment and does not include compilers or debuggers.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not a development environment and does not contain development tools such as compilers or debuggers.

evidence.claim: The JavaFX runtime is available separately and is not part of the JRE.
evidence.evidenceText: The JavaFX runtime is also available separately, and is not part of the JRE.

evidence.claim: Vendors must follow the Java SE Runtime Environment Binary Code License agreement.
evidence.evidenceText: Vendors must follow the terms of the Java SE Runtime Environment Binary Code License agreement.
evidence.pageTarget: Java SE Runtime Environment Binary Code License

evidence.claim: Beta and other pre-release versions may not be redistributed under the license described in the README.
evidence.evidenceText: NOTE - The license for this software does not allow the redistribution of beta and other pre-release versions.

evidence.claim: Optional files may be excluded from JRE redistributions at the vendor's discretion.
evidence.evidenceText: Optional files may be excluded from redistributions of the Java SE Runtime Environment at the vendor's discretion.
evidence.pageTarget: Java SE Runtime Environment required and optional files

evidence.claim: All files not listed as optional must be included in redistributions of the runtime environment.
evidence.evidenceText: All files not in these lists of optional files must be included in redistributions of the runtime environment.
evidence.pageTarget: Java SE Runtime Environment required and optional files

evidence.claim: The JCE policy files shipped with the JDK and JRE allow strong but limited cryptography due to import control restrictions for some countries.
evidence.evidenceText: Due to import control restrictions for some countries, the Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) policy files shipped with the Java SE Development Kit and the Java SE Runtime Environment allow strong but limited cryptography to be used.

evidence.claim: Root CA certificates may be added to or removed from the Java SE cacerts file.
evidence.evidenceText: Root CA certificates may be added to or removed from the Java SE certificate file located at <java-home>/lib/security/cacerts

evidence.claim: The Endorsed Standards Override Mechanism allows later versions of classes and interfaces implementing Endorsed Standards or Standalone Technologies to be incorporated into the Java Platform.
evidence.evidenceText: The Endorsed Standards Override Mechanism provides a means whereby later versions of classes and interfaces that implement Endorsed Standards or Standalone Technologies may be incorporated into the Java Platform.

evidence.claim: The Java Runtime Environment and JavaFX runtime are products of Oracle.
evidence.evidenceText: The Java(TM) Runtime Environment (JRE) and the JavaFX(TM) runtime are products of Oracle.
